item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.3 No.11, 1955
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text: TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No. 11 (47) Vol. III of a voyage round the world (r) UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES JAPANESE THEATRE
pages.handwritten_text:
NOH Drama
curiously [?] mask
be hidden real self/preson[?]
power in silence
Modern Theatre
sketch_descriptions: Expressive black ink sketch of a figure in elaborate traditional Japanese costume and headpiece, gesturing with an outstretched hand, likely a performer in Noh theatre.
Group sketch of several figures, some seated, appearing to be watching or discussing something, with one figure possibly in traditional attire, representing a scene from modern theatre.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle visually contrasts traditional Japanese Noh drama with modern Japanese theatre, capturing both in loose, expressive sketches and accompanying handwritten notes.

pages.handwritten_text: Kabuki drama audience Kabuki drama puppet theatre
sketch_descriptions: A series of figures sitting in an audience, some looking forward, others in profile, sketched with expressive line work.
Another clustered group of people sitting closely together, likely within an indoor setting, appearing attentive or contemplative.
A performer in dramatic costume, suggested to be part of a kabuki play, stands with head bowed.
A sequence of three puppeteers or actors handling a puppet with exaggerated gestures; labeled 'puppet theatre.'
A single figure wearing a heavy robe, hunched over, possibly an audience member or performer.
A procession or group carrying a banner or lantern, accompanied by several figures in traditional Japanese garb.
Several figures in various poses next to or interacting with one another, some seated, one figure turned rearward.
A close-up of three people in conversation, one looking out at the viewer, with distinct expressions presented.
An intimate kabuki drama scene—they appear engaged in emotional dialogue or performance, with one figure facing away.
A single figure viewed from the back, shoulders up, isolated from the rest.
pages.page_summary: This page captures lively sketches and handwritten notes depicting scenes from a kabuki drama, puppet theatre, and the audience observing these performances. The visual focus is on both the performers and spectators, rendered with expressive, animated line work.

pages.typeset_text: "TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 2s. 6d. A COPY YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE: £3. 3s. 0d (or equivalent). SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION. SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME. Handprinted in Great Britain by Poets & Painters' Press 146 Sutton Walk, S.E.1. from blocks made by Austin Melford and published from Bridge Arch 158, Sutton Walk, South Bank, London, S.E.1. Copyright reserved.
pages.handwritten_text: Geisha practice for autumn dance festival 36 Shimbashi Kabukiza (Chuo-Ku, Aswa Hof)[?]
sketch_descriptions: An expressive sketch in the top left showing a woman in traditional Japanese attire, likely a kimono, walking with a composed posture.
A fluid sketch in the top right of another woman in flowing robes, depicted mid-motion, possibly gesturing or dancing.

A detailed group scene occupying the bottom third of the page, showing several women in kimonos inside what appears to be a rehearsal or dressing room, with an audience or group of observers seated in the foreground, all engaged in conversation or observation.
pages.page_summary: This page captures scenes of women, likely geisha, preparing for or practicing for an autumn dance festival at Shimbashi Kabukiza in Chuo-Ku, rendered in Felix Topolski's expressive line work. The sketches convey both individual figures and a communal, behind-the-scenes rehearsal atmosphere.
